# Transcrição: 13. Survey

- **Fonte:** 13. Survey
- **Duração:** 02:21
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:21]

The exit survey, this is so strong. It's a follow up. You have given up on the clothes. What I want you to do now is I want you to go straight to an exit survey. I want you to have one printed up on a small sheet of paper with questions. It could be technology driven, but I would personally rather have you hand deliver it or I can sit on a mobile device. We've actually created this for mobile devices. Well, you can actually ask somebody on the way out. Can I ask just, you know, just a few questions before you leave? Did we have what you wanted? It's yes or no. See, I want to get this really black or white. Did we have what you wanted? Did we get you figures on it? Yes or no? Yes, you did. Were we within your budget? Yes or no? On a scale from one to 10, how would you rate our solution that we provided you? Okay, what is your biggest concern at this time? Maybe that's an open ended sentence. And what would we have to do to earn your business? Now, the first four questions are yes or no. And the last two are open ended. What is the biggest concern at this time? And what would we have to do to earn your business at this time? It's called an exit survey. It is a form of follow up. Some people also think this is a closing, another way to close. But the truth is, follow up is closing. Follow up is an attempt. Remember the definition. Follow up is the effort to complete an activity. And you hadn't completed the activity yet. So on the way out the door, not after they buy, I want to survey people. I want to survey the people that don't buy from me because the number is massive. And people that don't buy from you have more valuable survey data than people that did buy from you. Trust me. The people that don't buy from you, when surveyed will start giving you unbelievable data about what you have to do differently, about how you can effectively handle people differently. I've done this in industries where we wanted to figure out what was broken in their process and be able to increase their results. We have doubled companies. We've taken companies from the bottom of customer experience, evaluation forums, taken from the very bottom, all the way to the very top, with a simple exit survey that revealed data with unsold clients that converted more clients, that told us exactly what we needed to do different, told us what products we needed, what our pricing needed to be. Tried the exit survey. It can take a follow up, into a close, and do it at almost no cost.
